questions.question: A circle has center (3, −2) and passes through the point (7, 1). Write the equation of the circle in standard form.
questions.explanation: Find the radius using the distance formula: r = √[(7−3)² + (1−(−2))²] = √[16 + 9] = √25 = 5. The standard form is (x − h)² + (y − k)² = r², so (x − 3)² + (y + 2)² = 25.

questions.question: A sector of a circle with radius 6 has a central angle of 72°. Find the arc length of this sector.
The circle (x − 1)² + (y − 4)² = 16 has a tangent line at the point (5, 4). What is the slope of this tangent line?
questions.explanation: The center is (1, 4) and the point of tangency is (5, 4). The radius has slope (4−4)/(5−1) = 0 (horizontal). The tangent is perpendicular to the radius, so it is vertical with undefined slope.

questions.question: A circle with radius 10 has a sector with area 25π. What is the central angle of this sector in degrees?
Circle A has equation x² + y² = 36. Circle B has equation (x − 5)² + (y − 12)² = 9. Find the distance between the centers of the two circles.
questions.explanation: Center of Circle A is (0, 0) with radius 6. Center of Circle B is (5, 12) with radius 3. Distance = √[(5−0)² + (12−0)²] = √[25 + 144] = √169 = 13.

questions.question: A chord of a circle with radius 13 is 10 units long. What is the distance from the center of the circle to the chord?
questions.explanation: The perpendicular from the center to a chord bisects the chord. Form a right triangle with hypotenuse 13 (radius), one leg 5 (half the chord), and the other leg d (distance to chord). By Pythagorean theorem: d² + 5² = 13², so d² = 169 − 25 = 144, giving d = 12.
